MISSION LOG

EVENTS

JAN
17

Power and Energy Society 101

The IEEE IAS–PES ISTIC Student Branch Joint Chapter hosted an introductory online session on the IEEE Power & Energy Society (PES), providing students and young professionals with insights into the society’s focus areas, initiatives, and career opportunities in the energy sector.Presented by Ibtihel Trabelsi — PES Tunisia Section Chapter Secretary, PES SYP R8 Tunisia Zone Representative, Data Science & AI Master’s Student, and Renewable Energy Graduate.Attendees explored the world of power and energy engineering and learned how to get involved with IEEE PES for professional growth.

COMPLETEDSESSIONPES
›
JAN
19

Power And Energy Society Success Stories

The IEEE IAS–PES ISTIC Student Branch Joint Chapter hosted an engaging online session on leadership and innovation in the power and energy sector, giving students and young professionals a chance to learn from experienced industry leaders.Presented by Chehine Zouari, Ayoub Madyouni, and Molka Guesmi — trailblazers driving change and growth in the energy industry.Attendees gained valuable insights into career development, industry trends, and strategies for success in the energy sector.

COMPLETEDPanel DiscussionPES
›
FEB
4

Industrial Applications Society Success Stories

The IEEE IAS–PES ISTIC Student Branch Joint Chapter hosted an online session as part of the Industrial Applications Society Success Stories, giving students and young professionals an inside look at the journeys, challenges, and breakthroughs of leading industry professionals.Presented by Chehine Zouari, Tawfik Sallemi, and Eya Karmous — accomplished leaders in industrial applications and engineering innovation.Attendees gained actionable insights and inspiration from professionals shaping the industrial applications landscape, perfect for students and young engineers looking to advance their careers.

COMPLETEDPanel DiscussionIAS
›
FEB
8

Beyond Repair: The Era Of Self-Fixing Machines

The IEEE IAS–PES ISTIC Student Branch Joint Chapter hosted an interactive session on Predictive Maintenance, showing how AI and IoT are transforming industrial machinery into self-monitoring, self-fixing systems. Students and young professionals gained insights into cutting-edge technologies driving the smart industry.Presented by Ayoub Madyouni — Industry expert in automation and predictive maintenance.Attendees explored the logic behind predictive maintenance, learned about industry trends for 2026, and discovered practical insights for implementing smart factory solutions.

COMPLETEDWORKSHOPIAS
›
FEB
13

Programmable Logic Controller Workshop 101

The IEEE IAS–PES ISTIC Student Branch Joint Chapter hosted PLC Workshop 101, an interactive session designed to introduce students and young professionals to industrial control systems and hands-on programmable logic controller (PLC) applications.Presented by Yassin Maalaoui — Chair of the ISTIC RAS Chapter and specialist in industrial computing, bringing practical expertise and guidance for learners.Participants gained foundational PLC programming skills, insights into real-world industrial automation, and practical tips to enhance their engineering toolkit for future professional growth.

COMPLETEDWORKSHOPIAS
›
FEB
18

Beyond Reality: The Digital Twin Revolution Workshop

The IEEE IAS–PES ISTIC Student Branch Joint Chapter hosted an advanced session on Digital Twin Technology, guiding students and young professionals through the latest innovations in industrial automation and virtual process management.Presented by Ayoub Madyouni — Expert in Industrial Automation and AI-driven predictive maintenance.Attendees explored how digital twins mirror physical factories, enable real-time process simulation, and provide next-generation solutions for mechanical and power systems.

COMPLETEDWORKSHOPIAS
›
FEB
21

Ramadan IT 2026

The IEEE IAS–PES ISTIC Student Branch Joint Chapter hosted Ramadan-IT 2026, a technical bootcamp exploring the intersection of smart energy systems, artificial intelligence, and industrial innovation. Participants gained practical knowledge, insights from experts, and strategies to tackle real-world energy and industrial challenges.

COMPLETEDBOOTCAMPIASPES
›
FEB
23

Ramadan IT Session 1: AI To Smart Energy Consumption And Optimization

Led by Hamza Mellouli, this session introduced participants to AI-driven energy systems, covering smart forecasting, consumption analytics, and real-world optimization strategies.Attendees explored smart grids, intelligent algorithms, and data-driven energy decisions, building foundational skills in industrial AI and energy optimization.

COMPLETEDBOOTCAMPPES
›
FEB
25

Ramadan IT Session 2: Energy Systems & Smart Consumption

Eng. Mohamed Sief, Electrical Power Engineer at the Egyptian Electricity Transmission Company and IEEE volunteer, guided participants through the future of smart energy systems and sustainable solutions, including off-grid photovoltaic projects and micro-mobility initiatives.Attendees gained real-world insights into energy systems, sustainability, and impactful engineering leadership.

COMPLETEDBOOTCAMPPES
›
FEB
27

Ramadan IT Session 3: Industry 5.0 Unlocked: AI Digital Twins & The Future Of Smart Industry

Mohamed Iyadh Chehine Zouari explored the next production revolution, focusing on Industry 5.0, Digital Twins, AI, and Big Data to understand how human-centric innovation is reshaping modern industries.Participants learned the skills and strategies future engineers need to thrive in smart industrial environments.

COMPLETEDBOOTCAMPIAS
›

IEEE Industry Applications Society / Power & Energy Society Student Branch Joint Chapter. Empowering future engineers through innovation and collaboration.
